content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All five amendments identify real, verified defects introduced by the retry/backoff changes. Tests are broken by the retry limit increase (fr-1-2fc8a73c) and will be painfully slow in CI without paused time (fr-1-171fc893). The BinaryNotFound fast-fail goal is undermined by the availability path still using BackendUnavailable (fr-1-5008e57c). The backoff sleep ignores cancellation (fr-1-06c34f70). And timeout failures lose diagnostic artifacts that other failure modes preserve (fr-1-8b4ded53). Each amendment addresses a concrete correctness, performance, or observability gap.

## Votes

- `fr-1-2fc8a73c`: Accept
  Verified: all four tests use with_transient_failure(stage, 3) while TransportFailure max_attempts is now 5. With only 3 failures, the 4th attempt succeeds within the retry budget, so the first run completes successfully and assert!(first_result.is_err()) fails. Bumping to 5 transient failures exhausts the retry limit and restores the intended test behavior. This is a straightforward test breakage caused by the retry limit change.
- `fr-1-171fc893`: Accept
  Verified: all four tests use plain #[tokio::test] without start_paused = true, while other retry-related tests in the same file (retry_exhaustion_transitions_run_to_failed_state, cancellation_halts_retry_loop, etc.) already use start_paused = true. With exponential backoff of 5s + 10s + 20s across retries, each test incurs ~35 seconds of real wall-clock delay. Adding start_paused = true makes tokio::time::sleep advance instantly. This is a real CI performance issue.
- `fr-1-5008e57c`: Accept
  Verified: resolve_binary() at process_backend.rs:493-498 returns AppError::BackendUnavailable when the binary is not found. error.rs:302 maps BackendUnavailable to FailureClass::TransportFailure (retryable). agent_execution/service.rs:362-372 remaps availability errors to TransportFailure as well. So the common case of a missing claude/codex binary burns all 5 transport retries before failing. BinaryNotFound (non-retryable) is only reached via the rarer spawn-failure path (process_backend.rs:936-939) or exit-code-127 path (process_backend.rs:1381-1384). The fast-fail acceptance criterion requires BinaryNotFound to propagate through the availability/build-command path as well.
- `fr-1-06c34f70`: Accept
  Verified: engine.rs:4637 performs a plain tokio::time::sleep(backoff) without checking the cancellation token. The cancellation check at line 4561 gates whether to retry, but if cancellation arrives after that check (e.g., during the backoff sleep), the run sits idle for up to 60 seconds before the loop can notice. Using tokio::select! to race the sleep against cancellation_token.cancelled() is the standard pattern and would make shutdown responsive. This is a real correctness issue — shared mutable state (cancellation) is not properly observed.
- `fr-1-8b4ded53`: Accept
  Verified: the timeout branch at process_backend.rs:987-1001 returns an error from spawn_and_wait(), and invoke() handles it at lines 1161-1163 by calling prepared.cleanup(). For Codex, cleanup() at lines 55-63 calls best_effort_cleanup() which deletes temp files. In contrast, cleanup_failed_invocation() at lines 66-90 preserves artifacts in runtime/failed for debugging. A hung backend that times out is arguably MORE worth inspecting than a clean non-zero exit, yet the timeout path destroys the evidence. Routing timeout failures through the preserving cleanup path is the correct fix.
